QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_24

question.text: An immediate annuity differs from a deferred annuity in that:

answer.text: An immediate annuity starts paying income right away while a deferred annuity starts at a future date
An immediate annuity has higher returns than a deferred annuity
A deferred annuity pays more frequently than an immediate annuity
An immediate annuity requires lower investment than a deferred annuity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An immediate annuity begins paying income right after purchase (or within a short period), while a deferred annuity accumulates the corpus and starts paying income at a specified future date. **The Trap:** Higher returns (Option B) depends on market conditions, not the timing of income start. Payment frequency (Option C) is an option choice, not a defining difference. **Cross-Exam Context:** Annuity types are tested in Series XVII (NPS exit options) and X-B (insurance module). Understanding the timing difference is crucial for retirement income planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use immediate annuities for clients already in retirement who need income now. Deferred annuities work well in the pre-retirement stage to lock in annuity rates and guarantee future income.
